title: Ordinary-Use Pull Audit
goal: Check which Memory and Planning surfaces are actually used in ordinary work.
Identify low-pull surfaces that should be merged, demoted, or retired.
Make outsider-legibility and self-hosting bias explicit review dimensions for everyday surface choice.
non_goals: Re-open broader planning or architecture work beyond the pull audit.

review_mode.review question: Which Memory and Planning surfaces are actually used in ordinary work, which are skipped, and which low-pull surfaces should be merged, demoted, or retired?

findings.title: Feedback routing is support-only and fits memory better than a docs-level canonical page
findings.summary: The feedback-routing convention is a repeatable dogfooding procedure, not broad product doctrine. It belongs in memory/runbooks, where the repo keeps other local operator procedures with durable but narrow value.
findings.evidence: `docs/contributor-playbook.md` used the page as a capture convention; `docs/lazy-discovery-measurements.md` already treats the usage ledger as the durable ordinary-use record; the file itself was a narrow routing rubric rather than a user-facing contract.
findings.risk if unchanged: `docs/` keeps accumulating low-pull operational notes that are better owned as repo-local runbooks.
findings.suggested action: Move the capture convention into `.agentic-workspace/memory/repo/runbooks/`, delete the docs copy, and keep `docs/` focused on broader contracts and review artifacts.
findings.promotion trigger: immediate; the surface is low-pull and already has a better owner

findings.title: Ordinary-use pull needs explicit legibility and familiarity-bias questions
findings.summary: The usage ledger can already record which surface was chosen and why, but that is not enough to tell whether the choice was product-shaped or just insider-shaped.
findings.evidence: the ledger contract tracked goal, used surface, skipped surface, and follow-up, but it did not require a fresh-agent legibility note or a self-hosting-bias note.
findings.risk if unchanged: repeated skip patterns will remain difficult to interpret, and friction seen mainly by fresh or cheaper agents may stay underweighted.
findings.suggested action: Add legibility and bias prompts to the usage ledger and review mode so future entries can separate product fit from repository familiarity.
findings.promotion trigger: immediate; the audit lane should preserve those questions in the checked-in contract

findings.title: Review guidance needed a dedicated ordinary-use pull mode
findings.summary: The review portfolio covered context cost and generated-surface trust, but it did not have a dedicated lane for ordinary-use pull across Memory and Planning.
findings.evidence: the review catalog had no mode that explicitly asked which surfaces are used, skipped, or low-pull in day-to-day work.
findings.risk if unchanged: the same question could keep reappearing as ad hoc cleanup instead of a stable review lane.
findings.suggested action: Add an `ordinary-use-pull` review mode that names low-pull scaffolding, outsider legibility, and self-hosting bias as explicit findings.
findings.promotion trigger: immediate; review modes should match recurring review questions

recommendation.promote: the memory move and the new ordinary-use pull review lane.
recommendation.defer: broader surface consolidation until the next repeated pull signal appears.
recommendation.dismiss: any pressure to turn this into telemetry or a general analytics system.

drift_log: 2026-04-16: Review created during the simplification tranche for issues `#120`, `#115`, `#116`, and `#114`.
